pattern: A subtree inversion flips the sign of everything below it, so a tree DP carrying the current sign and the distance since the last inversion lets each node choose to invert (only when far enough) for the maximum total.
hints: Inverting a node negates its whole subtree, so a node's final sign depends only on the parity of how many of its ancestors-or-self were inverted; track that parity as you descend.
The only restriction is that an inverted node and an inverted ancestor must be at least k edges apart, so also carry how many edges have passed since the last inversion on this path.

Per node compute the best subtree total for each (incoming sign, distance-since-last-inversion capped at k): either skip inverting (children inherit the sign and distance+1) or, when the distance has reached k, invert (children flip sign and reset distance to 1).

edge_inputs.description: k larger than tree height allows leaf inversions only
all positive values, inverting only hurts
k=1 lets adjacent inversions, parity matters

stress.description: random rooted tree with mixed-sign values and small k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    edges = [[rng.randint(0, i - 1), i] for i in range(1, n)]
    nums = [rng.randint(-200, 200) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 6)
    return [edges, nums, k]
stress.note: random parents keep the tree shallow so the recursive oracle does not overflow
